thesis_bull: Held since 2026-05-12 entry on rank-3 momentum (6m +25.97%); position currently +10.41% unrealized (mark $101.19 vs entry $91.6483). Underlying momentum thesis intact (no EXIT signal from lib.signals; not breaching stop or target). Closing pre-emptively here forfeits the next-day continuation of the trend — if earnings are positive we re-establish on the EOD signal at tomorrow's close.
thesis_bear: CSCO reports Q3 FY26 earnings TODAY 2026-05-13 AMC (after market close). This is a single-stock idiosyncratic catalyst with no diversifying counterweight in the book; options market implies a ~9.87% one-day move on the print (per TipRanks earnings preview). A 10% gap-down would push CSCO to ~$91, near our $91.65 entry — wiping the +10% unrealized gain and consuming roughly half the per-trade stop budget on a single overnight event we can see coming. The trade was sized as a momentum carry, not an earnings-window play. Pre-close is exactly the routine designed to refuse this kind of asymmetric overnight exposure.
invalidation_condition: Earnings catalyst removed: Cisco postpones the Q3 FY26 release. Per Cisco IR press release dated 2026-04 (cited below) the release is scheduled for 2026-05-13 AMC. As of routine timestamp 19:36 UTC (15:36 ET, ~24 minutes pre-close) no postponement has been published. If Cisco postpones, this overnight-risk-driven close becomes unnecessary and we re-enter on the EOD ENTRY confirmation.
confidence_notes: High confidence in the close: the catalyst is verified (multiple sources, see provenance), the asymmetry is real (sized as momentum carry, exposed to earnings-window gap), and pre_close.md explicitly mandates this behaviour for known overnight catalysts. The 0.15 residual uncertainty covers: (a) the possibility that today's late-day quote source has a feed-version mismatch like yesterday's IEX bar regression, (b) the possibility that Cisco delays the release, and (c) general regret that we may close into a positive print and forgo upside — accepted by routine design.
risk_manager_notes: Inline risk-manager review at 19:36 UTC: symbol CSCO is in watchlist.yaml with approved_for_paper_trading=true; strategy large_cap_momentum_top5 is in allowed_strategies (no disallowed flag); decision class PAPER_CLOSE is exit-side so per-trade R/R, sizing caps, sector-correlation caps, and total-exposure caps do not apply (closing reduces exposure). Daily-loss check: realized loss from this close = $-2.59 (1 cent slippage round-trip on 65 shares against +10.41% MTM gain — net realized pnl approximately +$586 after slippage). Daily PnL utilization remains 0% on the daily-loss limit. Trades-today: 1/5 after this close, under the cap. Open positions after: 4/8, under the cap. Data freshness: 0.7s, well under 60s staleness limit. No permission flags involved (no margin, options, shorts, leverage). Verdict: APPROVED.
compliance_notes: Inline compliance review at 19:36 UTC: mode PAPER_TRADING permits PAPER_CLOSE; CSCO approved for paper trading; strategy in allowed list; risk_manager_verdict=APPROVED; bull/bear/invalidation all present and non-empty; sources cited with URLs in data_provenance; schema-compliant (all required fields present); no live execution class. Verdict: APPROVED.

data_provenance.earnings_catalyst_summary: Cisco Q3 FY26 earnings release scheduled 2026-05-13 AMC (after close, 4:30 PM ET conference call). Period ending 2026-04-25. Multiple independent sources concur.
data_provenance.regime: bullish_trend (medium confidence; partly feed-artifact per pre_market journal note)
data_provenance.circuit_breaker_state: OUT (peak inflated artifact; closes are not throttled by exposure_fraction(OUT)=0.0 — that throttles new opens only)
data_provenance.routine_rationale: pre_close.md Step 9 — 'any open position with material overnight risk → propose PAPER_CLOSE with reason overnight_risk: <event>'. Reason: overnight_risk: CSCO Q3 FY26 earnings AMC tonight.
